coverage_contract.problem_pattern: Questions ask for the color of a specific referenced object in an image, where success depends on isolating that object and reading its dominant color rather than using global scene cues.
supported_variations: Multiple-choice questions asking the color of a named object like handbag, swim ring, shirt, car, or sign
Direct attribute lookup where the target object is visible but occupies only a localized region of the image
unsupported_variations: Questions requiring fine-grained material, pattern, brand, or text recognition instead of basic localized color identification
forbidden_case_specific_assumptions: Do not hardcode colors associated with common objects or scenes
Do not assume the target object is centered, large, or uniquely salient
Do not use one-off coordinates, boxes, or image-specific crop positions from representative examples
coverage_contract.why_this_should_generalize: Most failures concentrate on direct color queries over localized objects, a stable and highly reusable visual primitive that can support many object-color lookup questions across the family.

rewrite_requirements: Write the artifact file before returning.
Keep ToolResult.artifacts aligned with real relative file paths.
